originalTitle: Australian man charged with murder after dead girl found in suitcase in Thailand
summary: The 17-year-old's body was found discarded near a railway track on Saturday, police say.
content:
Simon Peter Carman was arrested at Bangkok's Suvarnabhumi Airport in the early hours of Saturday, police said

An Australian man has been charged with murder after the body of a 17-year-old girl was found in a suitcase in Thailand, local and Australian media report.

Police in the coastal city of Pattaya said they had found the teenager "stuffed" in the bag, which had been discarded near a railway track, in the early hours of Saturday.

Thai police said they arrested Simon Peter Carman at Bangkok's Suvarnabhumi Airport in connection with the death as he was allegedly "preparing to flee the country".

He denies the charges, according to reports. In a message issued to the victim's family after his arrest, Carman said: "I feel bad for what happened to your daughter. It was out of my control."

Pattaya City Police said the 17-year-old, named in local media as Tunchanok Donhomla, had been reported missing at 17:00 local time (12:00 GMT) on Friday.

In a statement on social media, the force said it reviewed CCTV footage which allegedly showed Carman entering a condominium with her before later emerging alone "carrying a large suitcase".

It said he loaded the bag onto a motorbike before driving towards a railway line.

Officers questioned and arrested Carman at the airport in the Thai capital, some 150km (93 miles) north of Pattaya, at 01:15 on Saturday.

The teenager's naked body was found in a suitcase some 15 minutes later, the force said.

An image of the victim shared by her father and step-mother with the Reuters news agency

According to reports, Carman denied murder and further charges related to moving or concealing a body and taking a minor for sexual purposes, and claimed he had acted in self defence.

In a video recorded while he was in custody, the suspect issued a message to the victim's family, saying: "I feel bad for what happened to your daughter. It was out of my control."

"I know you'll be very sad, upset… same [as] me."

He added: "Please tell other girls… just to be careful."

The victim's father said he was "deeply saddened" by his daughter's death, while her step-mother said: "I just want him to face the full consequences."
chineseTitle: 澳大利亚男子在泰国手提箱中发现死去女孩后被控谋杀
chineseSummary: 警方称，周六，这名 17 岁男子的尸体被发现被丢弃在铁轨附近。
chineseContent:
警方称，西蒙·彼得·卡曼于周六凌晨在曼谷素万那普机场被捕

据当地和澳大利亚媒体报道，在泰国的一个手提箱中发现一名 17 岁女孩的尸体后，一名澳大利亚男子被指控犯有谋杀罪。

沿海城市芭堤雅警方表示，周六凌晨，他们在被丢弃在铁轨附近的袋子中发现了这名少年“塞满”的东西。

泰国警方表示，他们在曼谷素万那普机场逮捕了与这起死亡事件有关的西蒙·彼得·卡曼，因为据称他“准备逃离该国”。

据报道，他否认这些指控。卡曼被捕后发给受害者家人的信息中说：“我对你女儿的遭遇感到难过。这超出了我的控制范围。”

芭堤雅市警方表示，当地媒体称这名 17 岁男子 Tunchanok Donhomla 于当地时间周五 17:00（格林尼治标准时间 12:00）失踪。

在社交媒体上的一份声明中，警方表示，他们查看了闭路电视录像，据称该录像显示卡曼与她一起进入公寓，然后独自“拎着一个大手提箱”出现。

据称，他将包装上摩托车，然后驶向铁路线。

周六凌晨 1 点 15 分，警察在芭堤雅以北约 150 公里（93 英里）的泰国首都机场审问并逮捕了卡曼。

警方称，大约 15 分钟后，在一个手提箱中发现了这名少年赤裸的尸体。

受害者的父亲和继母向路透社分享了受害者的照片

据报道，卡曼否认谋杀以及与移动或隐藏尸体以及以性为目的带走未成年人有关的进一步指控，并声称他的行为是出于自卫。

在被拘留期间录制的一段视频中，嫌疑人向受害者家属发出了一条信息，称：“我对你女儿的遭遇感到难过。这超出了我的控制范围。”

“我知道你会非常悲伤、沮丧……和我一样。”

他补充道：“请告诉其他女孩……要小心。”

受害者的父亲表示，他对女儿的死感到“深感悲痛”，而她的继母则表示：“我只是想让他面对全部后果。”
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
